node_summary.purpose: Semantic HTML5 structure defining the complete page layout with navigation, 7 content sections, and footer. Provides the document skeleton that CSS styles and JavaScript enhances.
node_summary.behavior: 1. Navigation renders as fixed overlay with logo and section links 2. Hero section displays centered content with call-to-action buttons 3. About section presents two-column layout with image placeholder and bio 4. Services section shows 6 offering cards in responsive grid 5. Process section displays 4-step workflow visualization 6. Testimonials section shows single client quote 7. Contact section renders multi-field inquiry form 8. Footer provides site navigation, contact info, and social links

key_data_flows.input: Anchor hrefs (#about, #services, etc.) enable section jumping
Form fields collect: name, email, phone, service type, message
key_data_flows.output: Form action sends data to contact@anlystudio.com
Phone link enables direct dialing on mobile

side_effects.effect: Tel link may trigger phone dialer on mobile devices

section.name: Navigation
section.elements: nav, a.logo, ul.nav-links with 4 li items

section.name: Hero
section.content: Subtitle, h1 title, description paragraph, 2 CTA buttons

section.name: About
section.elements: section.about, div.about-image with frame and accent, div.about-content
section.data: Artist name: April Johnson, Location: Ladysmith, Wisconsin

section.name: Process
section.elements: section.process, div.section-header, div.process-steps with 4 process-step items

section.name: Contact
section.elements: section.contact, h2, p, form.contact-form with 5 form-groups

edge_cases.case: Social links have empty href="#" - non-functional placeholders
Form enctype="text/plain" may cause formatting issues in mailto
No mobile menu - nav-links hidden at 768px with no alternative

performance.metric: Proper use of section, nav, footer, h1-h3

security.item: Email exposed in mailto: and footer link
Phone number exposed in tel: link
No CSRF protection on form (mailto doesn't require it)

accessibility.item: Form labels properly associated with inputs via for/id
No skip-to-content link for keyboard users
No ARIA landmarks beyond implicit roles
Mobile nav hidden without hamburger menu alternative
